title: Categorical encoding tradeoffs
description: How ordinal, one-hot, target, and embedding encodings trade dimensionality, leakage risk, and model fit.
body: Use when selecting a categorical encoding. Ordinal injects a false order unless the field is truly ordered; one-hot is safe but explodes width on high cardinality; target encoding is compact but leaks unless computed out-of-fold; embeddings capture similarity but need a neural model and enough data. Do not target-encode in-fold. Actions: weigh cardinality, model family, and leakage risk, then pick and verify on validation. Validation signals include a dimensionality check and a leakage-free encoder fit out-of-fold. Risk boundary: an in-fold target encoder produces optimistic metrics that collapse in production; if statistics were fit on all rows, refit out-of-fold before trusting the result.
